sourceRefs.summary: First-pass bounded reproduction of stage-specific Table S3 counts.
Evidence matrix separating table-level reproduction from missing PRDM9, NOMe, and fate evidence.
Worker C boundary: operational/data gaps are not scientific contradictions.

rationale: Using only existing local evidence, the stage-specific de novo H3K4me3 peak counts and DSB-hotspot-associated counts from Supplementary Table S3 are reproduced at table level. The broader PRDM9 binding, NOMe/open-chromatin, and CO/NCO fate interpretation remains insufficiently evidenced in this pass because the required interval files, signal matrices, direct fate outcomes, and original source code were not materialized.

supportingEvidenceRefs.summary: Counts match the paper-reported Table S3/Fig. 2 stage numbers for five locally available stages.
Evidence matrix row marking the stage-specific H3K4me3 count check as partially reproduced.

missingEvidence.summary: PRDM9 affinity-seq binding intervals or motif-analysis outputs were not locally materialized, so the reported 11716/16540 PRDM9-binding overlap was not reproduced.
Four-class H3K4me3 half-life reconstruction and known CO hotspot overlap were not reproduced, so the CO/NCO fate model is not directly supported by this run.
NOMe-seq GCH methylation matrices, NDR calls, and accessibility-ratio statistics were not available in the local subset.
Original 2020 source code is request-only, limiting exact reproduction of filtering, overlap, and plotting decisions.

notes: Conservative conclusion: partial supplement-table reproduction, insufficient evidence for the full DSB fate mechanism, and no scientific negative result yet.
